refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: supporting teleoperation with communication quality that meets a specified packet loss limit. It does not combine multiple requirements or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to control packet loss
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between protocol parameters and teleoperation control loops
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality control within the protocol layer, supporting independence from other system functions. Adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms can be designed modularly to meet packet loss targets without tightly coupling to other system components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and do not degrade unexpectedly
Risk that adaptive mechanisms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes error correction and retransmission can be tuned to meet ≤1% packet loss without excessive overhead
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency and jitter constraints in the FR to ensure teleoperation responsiveness alongside packet loss targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication protocols and adaptive error correction mechanisms within the system

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving data packets over the network, supporting adaptive retransmission and error correction.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides status and performance metrics of the communication protocol to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as NGHE Solution is focused on network communication protocols; minimal diffusion as error correction and retransmission are core to this module.
Low cohesion since Vehicle module is more about physical vehicle control; high diffusion as communication protocol would need to interact with NGHE and Teleoperation.
Moderate cohesion since Teleoperation depends on communication but does not implement protocol details; moderate diffusion due to reliance on NGHE Solution.
Low cohesion as Safety Services focus on safety alerts, not communication protocols; high diffusion if protocol implemented here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it specializes in network communication and can encapsulate error correction and retransmission logic with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for tasking and control data may cause some coupling.
If the protocol requires safety-related adaptations, coordination with Safety Services might increase coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, fulfilling the functional requirement of supporting teleoperation with required communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for tasking and control data may cause some coupling.
Coordination with Safety Services might increase coupling if safety-related adaptations are needed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 9) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. Other modules have lower cohesion and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface, ensuring packet loss remains ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces for network communication and telemetry, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation to manage coupling and dependencies.
Establish coordination protocols with Safety Services if safety-related communication adaptations are required to minimize diffusion risk.
Include verification procedures in the Telemetry interface specification to ensure packet loss metrics are accurately reported and monitored.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The DP directly fulfills the FR with an adaptive communication protocol ensuring required packet loss limits, and the DP is well contained within a single IM with high cohesion and low diffusion. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although some coupling risks exist, they are manageable through recommended revisions rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and well-verified design at this level is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation to manage coupling and dependencies, establish coordination protocols with Safety Services to minimize diffusion risk, and include verification procedures in the Telemetry interface specification to ensure accurate monitoring of packet loss metrics.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol as DP fully satisfies the FR under specified network conditions.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for implementing the DP.
Verification through telemetry monitoring is sufficient to ensure compliance with packet loss requirements.
open_risks: Potential coupling between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules due to tasking and control data dependencies.
Possible increased coupling with Safety Services if safety-related communication adaptations are needed.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.
Coordination protocols with Safety Services if applicable.
Verification procedures and telemetry data demonstrating packet loss ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
